questions.question: Which of the following represents the Density of a material?
questions.explanation: Density is the ratio of mass to volume. It is a measure of material's compactness. Density = Mass/Volume

questions.question: Which of the following is the S.I. unit of density?
questions.explanation: The SI unit of density is kg m-3. It is a positive scalar quantity. The Gaussian unit of density is the gram per cubic centimeter which is equivalent to a gram per milliliter [1 g/cm3 = 1 g/ml].

questions.question: What is Specific Gravity of a liquid?

options.label: A
options.text: ratio of the density of a liquid to the density of water

options.label: B
options.text: product of the density of a substance to the density of water

options.label: C
options.text: ratio of the density of water to the density of a liquid

options.label: D
options.text: ratio of density of the liquid to the mass of the equal density of water

answer.label: A
answer.text: ratio of the density of a liquid to the density of water

questions.explanation: Specific gravity of a liquid is the ratio of the density of a substance to the density of water. The density of liquid water at standard conditions on earth is approximately 1,000 kg/m3.

questions.question: What is the unit of Specific gravity?
questions.explanation: Specific gravity is a unitless quantity. Specific gravity is the ratio of two same dimensions. The dimensions of density is M1 L- 3

questions.question: What is the density of water at 4°?
questions.explanation: The density of water at 4°C (277 K) is 1.0 × 103 kg m–3. The relative density of a substance is the ratio of its density to the density of water at 4° C.
